Steiner Tree - 50KV, 100 terminals

Approach: Here is the complete C++ code for solving the Steiner Tree problem using the MST heuristic:

View C++ Code (13253 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <limits>
#include <algorithm>
#include <set>
#include <map> // For metric closure graph

// Structure to represent an edge in the original graph
struct Edge {
    int to;
    int weight;
};

// Structure to represent a node in the priority queue for Dijkstra's
struct State {
    int vertex;
    int cost;

    bool operator>(const State& other) const {
        return cost > other.cost;
    }
};

// Function to parse input and build the graph
void parse_input(int& num_vertices, int& num_edges, int& num_terminals,
                 std::vector<std::vector<Edge>>& adj, std::vector<int>& terminals) {
    std::cin >> num_vertices >> num_edges >> num_terminals;

    adj.resize(num_vertices);
    for (int i = 0; i < num_edges; ++i) {
        int u, v, weight;
        std::cin >> u >> v >> weight;
        adj[u].push_back({v, weight});
        adj[v].push_back({u, weight}); // Undirected graph
    }

    terminals.resize(num_terminals);
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        std::cin >> terminals[i];
    }
}

// Dijkstra's algorithm to find shortest paths from a source vertex
// Returns distances and predecessors
std::pair<std::vector<long long>, std::vector<int>> dijkstra(int start_node, int num_vertices, const std::vector<std::vector<Edge>>& adj) {
    std::vector<long long> dist(num_vertices, std::numeric_limits<long long>::max());
    std::vector<int> pred(num_vertices, -1);
    std::priority_queue<State, std::vector<State>, std::greater<State>> pq;

    dist[start_node] = 0;
    pq.push({start_node, 0});

    while (!pq.empty()) {
        State current = pq.top();
        pq.pop();

        int u = current.vertex;
        int d = current.cost;

        if (d > dist[u]) {
            continue;
        }

        for (const auto& edge : adj[u]) {
            int v = edge.to;
            int weight = edge.weight;
            if (dist[u] + weight < dist[v]) {
                dist[v] = dist[u] + weight;
                pred[v] = u;
                pq.push({v, (int)dist[v]});
            }
        }
    }
    return {dist, pred};
}

// Function to reconstruct the path from start to end using predecessors
// Stores edges in the `path_edges` set. Edges are stored as pairs of {min_vertex, max_vertex} to handle undirected nature.
void reconstruct_path(int start, int end, const std::vector<int>& pred,
                      const std::vector<std::vector<Edge>>& adj,
                      std::set<std::tuple<int, int, int>>& path_edges,
                      const std::vector<long long>& dist) {
    if (dist[end] == std::numeric_limits<long long>::max()) {
        // No path exists
        return;
    }

    int current = end;
    while (current != start && current != -1) {
        int p = pred[current];
        if (p == -1) break; // Should not happen if dist[end] is not max

        // Find the weight of the edge (p, current)
        int weight = -1;
        for (const auto& edge : adj[p]) {
            if (edge.to == current) {
                weight = edge.weight;
                break;
            }
        }
        // Ensure edge is added in a canonical form (smaller vertex first)
        path_edges.insert({std::min(p, current), std::max(p, current), weight});
        current = p;
    }
}

// Structure for MST edges
struct MSTEdge {
    int u, v, weight;
};

// Comparator for MST edges for sorting (used in Kruskal's or Prim's with vector)
bool compareMSTEdges(const MSTEdge& a, const MSTEdge& b) {
    return a.weight < b.weight;
}

// Prim's algorithm for Minimum Spanning Tree
// Takes an adjacency list representation of the graph and returns a vector of MST edges.
// `metric_closure_adj` maps terminal ID to its neighbors and weights.
std::vector<MSTEdge> prim_mst(int num_terminals, const std::map<int, std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>>& metric_closure_adj, const std::vector<int>& terminals_map_idx_to_id) {
    std::vector<MSTEdge> result_mst_edges;
    if (num_terminals == 0) return result_mst_edges;

    // Map to store minimum edge weight to connect a vertex to the MST
    std::vector<long long> min_edge_weight(num_terminals, std::numeric_limits<long long>::max());
    // Vector to store the parent of each vertex in the MST
    std::vector<int> parent(num_terminals, -1);
    // Boolean array to track vertices included in MST
    std::vector<bool> in_mst(num_terminals, false);

    // Start with the first terminal (index 0)
    min_edge_weight[0] = 0;

    // The MST will have num_terminals vertices
    for (int count = 0; count < num_terminals; ++count) {
        int u_idx = -1;
        long long min_val = std::numeric_limits<long long>::max();

        // Find the vertex with the minimum edge weight among those not yet included in MST
        for (int v_idx = 0; v_idx < num_terminals; ++v_idx) {
            if (!in_mst[v_idx] && min_edge_weight[v_idx] < min_val) {
                min_val = min_edge_weight[v_idx];
                u_idx = v_idx;
            }
        }

        // If no vertex can be added, break (e.g., disconnected graph, though metric closure should be connected)
        if (u_idx == -1) break;

        in_mst[u_idx] = true;

        // Add the edge to the result if it's not the first vertex
        if (parent[u_idx] != -1) {
            result_mst_edges.push_back({terminals_map_idx_to_id[parent[u_idx]], terminals_map_idx_to_id[u_idx], (int)min_val});
        }

        // Update edge weights of adjacent vertices
        int u_terminal_id = terminals_map_idx_to_id[u_idx];
        if (metric_closure_adj.count(u_terminal_id)) {
            for (const auto& edge_pair : metric_closure_adj.at(u_terminal_id)) {
                int v_terminal_id = edge_pair.first;
                int weight = edge_pair.second;

                // Find the index of v_terminal_id in terminals_map_idx_to_id
                int v_idx = -1;
                for(int i = 0; i < terminals_map_idx_to_id.size(); ++i) {
                    if (terminals_map_idx_to_id[i] == v_terminal_id) {
                        v_idx = i;
                        break;
                    }
                }

                if (v_idx != -1 && !in_mst[v_idx] && weight < min_edge_weight[v_idx]) {
                    parent[v_idx] = u_idx;
                    min_edge_weight[v_idx] = weight;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return result_mst_edges;
}


int main() {
    // Fast I/O
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int num_vertices, num_edges, num_terminals;
    std::vector<std::vector<Edge>> adj;
    std::vector<int> terminals;

    parse_input(num_vertices, num_edges, num_terminals, adj, terminals);

    if (num_terminals <= 1) {
        // If 0 or 1 terminal, the Steiner tree is trivial
        if (num_terminals == 1) {
            std::cout << "0 0\n"; // Weight 0, 0 edges
        } else {
            std::cout << "0 0\n";
        }
        return 0;
    }

    // Store shortest path distances and predecessors from each terminal
    std::vector<std::vector<long long>> terminal_dists(num_terminals, std::vector<long long>(num_vertices));
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> terminal_preds(num_terminals, std::vector<int>(num_vertices));
    
    // Map to create a compact index for terminals in Prim's
    // Key: terminal ID, Value: index (0 to num_terminals-1)
    std::map<int, int> terminal_id_to_idx;
    // Vector to map index back to terminal ID
    std::vector<int> terminals_map_idx_to_id(num_terminals);

    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        terminal_id_to_idx[terminals[i]] = i;
        terminals_map_idx_to_id[i] = terminals[i];
        auto result = dijkstra(terminals[i], num_vertices, adj);
        terminal_dists[i] = result.first;
        terminal_preds[i] = result.second;
    }

    // Build the metric closure graph
    // Adjacency list for the metric closure graph: map terminal ID to list of {neighbor_terminal_ID, weight}
    std::map<int, std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>> metric_closure_adj;
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        for (int j = i + 1; j < num_terminals; ++j) {
            int u_term = terminals[i];
            int v_term = terminals[j];
            long long dist = terminal_dists[i][v_term]; // Distance from terminal i to terminal j

            if (dist != std::numeric_limits<long long>::max()) {
                metric_closure_adj[u_term].push_back({v_term, (int)dist});
                metric_closure_adj[v_term].push_back({u_term, (int)dist});
            }
        }
    }

    // Compute MST on the metric closure graph using Prim's algorithm
    std::vector<MSTEdge> metric_mst = prim_mst(num_terminals, metric_closure_adj, terminals_map_idx_to_id);

    // Collect all unique edges from the paths corresponding to the MST edges
    std::set<std::tuple<int, int, int>> steiner_tree_edges; // {u, v, weight}, sorted u, v
    
    for (const auto& mst_edge : metric_mst) {
        int u_term = mst_edge.u;
        int v_term = mst_edge.v;
        
        // Find which terminal index corresponds to u_term and v_term
        int u_term_idx = terminal_id_to_idx[u_term];
        // int v_term_idx = terminal_id_to_idx[v_term]; // Not directly used in reconstruct_path call

        // Reconstruct path using the correct predecessor array (based on which terminal was source)
        // The distance from u_term to v_term is given by terminal_dists[u_term_idx][v_term].
        // The predecessors for this path are stored in terminal_preds[u_term_idx].
        reconstruct_path(u_term, v_term, terminal_preds[u_term_idx], adj, steiner_tree_edges, terminal_dists[u_term_idx]);
    }

    // Union of edges might contain cycles. Compute a final MST on these collected edges.
    // First, collect all unique vertices involved in the steiner_tree_edges
    std::set<int> steiner_vertices_set;
    for (const auto& edge_tuple : steiner_tree_edges) {
        steiner_vertices_set.insert(std::get<0>(edge_tuple));
        steiner_vertices_set.insert(std::get<1>(edge_tuple));
    }
    
    // Convert set of vertices to a vector and create a map for new indices for MST
    std::vector<int> steiner_vertices(steiner_vertices_set.begin(), steiner_vertices_set.end());
    std::map<int, int> steiner_vertex_id_to_idx;
    for(int i = 0; i < steiner_vertices.size(); ++i) {
        steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[steiner_vertices[i]] = i;
    }

    // Build adjacency list for the subgraph formed by steiner_tree_edges
    // This is not directly used in Kruskal's but can be useful for debugging or other MST algos.
    // std::vector<std::vector<Edge>> steiner_subgraph_adj(steiner_vertices.size()); // Not needed for Kruskal's implementation below
    std::vector<MSTEdge> final_mst_candidate_edges;
    for (const auto& edge_tuple : steiner_tree_edges) {
        int u = std::get<0>(edge_tuple);
        int v = std::get<1>(edge_tuple);
        int weight = std::get<2>(edge_tuple);
        
        // int u_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[u]; // Not needed for Kruskal's candidate edges
        // int v_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[v]; // Not needed for Kruskal's candidate edges
        
        // steiner_subgraph_adj[u_idx].push_back({v_idx, weight}); // Not needed
        // steiner_subgraph_adj[v_idx].push_back({u_idx, weight}); // Not needed
        final_mst_candidate_edges.push_back({u, v, weight});
    }

    // Sort edges for Kruskal's algorithm
    std::sort(final_mst_candidate_edges.begin(), final_mst_candidate_edges.end(), compareMSTEdges);

    // Kruskal's algorithm for the final MST
    // Disjoint Set Union (DSU) structure
    std::vector<int> dsu_parent(steiner_vertices.size());
    std::vector<int> dsu_rank(steiner_vertices.size(), 0);
    for(int i = 0; i < steiner_vertices.size(); ++i) {
        dsu_parent[i] = i;
    }

    auto find_set = [&](auto self, int v_idx) -> int {
        if (v_idx == dsu_parent[v_idx])
            return v_idx;
        return dsu_parent[v_idx] = self(self, dsu_parent[v_idx]);
    };

    auto union_sets = [&](int a_idx, int b_idx) -> bool {
        a_idx = find_set(find_set, a_idx);
        b_idx = find_set(find_set, b_idx);
        if (a_idx != b_idx) {
            if (dsu_rank[a_idx] < dsu_rank[b_idx]) std::swap(a_idx, b_idx);
            dsu_parent[b_idx] = a_idx;
            if (dsu_rank[a_idx] == dsu_rank[b_idx]) dsu_rank[a_idx]++;
            return true;
        }
        return false;
    };

    std::vector<MSTEdge> final_steiner_tree;
    long long total_weight = 0;
    int edges_count = 0;

    for (const auto& edge : final_mst_candidate_edges) {
        int u_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[edge.u];
        int v_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[edge.v];

        if (union_sets(u_idx, v_idx)) {
            final_steiner_tree.push_back(edge);
            total_weight += edge.weight;
            edges_count++;
        }
    }

    // The problem statement guarantees a connected graph for terminals.
    // The resulting graph should connect all terminals.

    // Output
    std::cout << total_weight << " " << edges_count << "\n";
    for (const auto& edge : final_steiner_tree) {
        std::cout << edge.u << " " << edge.v << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

[100KV, 2M edges] Weight mismatch

Steiner Tree - 100KV, 2M edges

Approach: Here is the complete C++ code for solving the Steiner Tree problem using the MST heuristic:

View C++ Code (13253 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <limits>
#include <algorithm>
#include <set>
#include <map> // For metric closure graph

// Structure to represent an edge in the original graph
struct Edge {
    int to;
    int weight;
};

// Structure to represent a node in the priority queue for Dijkstra's
struct State {
    int vertex;
    int cost;

    bool operator>(const State& other) const {
        return cost > other.cost;
    }
};

// Function to parse input and build the graph
void parse_input(int& num_vertices, int& num_edges, int& num_terminals,
                 std::vector<std::vector<Edge>>& adj, std::vector<int>& terminals) {
    std::cin >> num_vertices >> num_edges >> num_terminals;

    adj.resize(num_vertices);
    for (int i = 0; i < num_edges; ++i) {
        int u, v, weight;
        std::cin >> u >> v >> weight;
        adj[u].push_back({v, weight});
        adj[v].push_back({u, weight}); // Undirected graph
    }

    terminals.resize(num_terminals);
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        std::cin >> terminals[i];
    }
}

// Dijkstra's algorithm to find shortest paths from a source vertex
// Returns distances and predecessors
std::pair<std::vector<long long>, std::vector<int>> dijkstra(int start_node, int num_vertices, const std::vector<std::vector<Edge>>& adj) {
    std::vector<long long> dist(num_vertices, std::numeric_limits<long long>::max());
    std::vector<int> pred(num_vertices, -1);
    std::priority_queue<State, std::vector<State>, std::greater<State>> pq;

    dist[start_node] = 0;
    pq.push({start_node, 0});

    while (!pq.empty()) {
        State current = pq.top();
        pq.pop();

        int u = current.vertex;
        int d = current.cost;

        if (d > dist[u]) {
            continue;
        }

        for (const auto& edge : adj[u]) {
            int v = edge.to;
            int weight = edge.weight;
            if (dist[u] + weight < dist[v]) {
                dist[v] = dist[u] + weight;
                pred[v] = u;
                pq.push({v, (int)dist[v]});
            }
        }
    }
    return {dist, pred};
}

// Function to reconstruct the path from start to end using predecessors
// Stores edges in the `path_edges` set. Edges are stored as pairs of {min_vertex, max_vertex} to handle undirected nature.
void reconstruct_path(int start, int end, const std::vector<int>& pred,
                      const std::vector<std::vector<Edge>>& adj,
                      std::set<std::tuple<int, int, int>>& path_edges,
                      const std::vector<long long>& dist) {
    if (dist[end] == std::numeric_limits<long long>::max()) {
        // No path exists
        return;
    }

    int current = end;
    while (current != start && current != -1) {
        int p = pred[current];
        if (p == -1) break; // Should not happen if dist[end] is not max

        // Find the weight of the edge (p, current)
        int weight = -1;
        for (const auto& edge : adj[p]) {
            if (edge.to == current) {
                weight = edge.weight;
                break;
            }
        }
        // Ensure edge is added in a canonical form (smaller vertex first)
        path_edges.insert({std::min(p, current), std::max(p, current), weight});
        current = p;
    }
}

// Structure for MST edges
struct MSTEdge {
    int u, v, weight;
};

// Comparator for MST edges for sorting (used in Kruskal's or Prim's with vector)
bool compareMSTEdges(const MSTEdge& a, const MSTEdge& b) {
    return a.weight < b.weight;
}

// Prim's algorithm for Minimum Spanning Tree
// Takes an adjacency list representation of the graph and returns a vector of MST edges.
// `metric_closure_adj` maps terminal ID to its neighbors and weights.
std::vector<MSTEdge> prim_mst(int num_terminals, const std::map<int, std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>>& metric_closure_adj, const std::vector<int>& terminals_map_idx_to_id) {
    std::vector<MSTEdge> result_mst_edges;
    if (num_terminals == 0) return result_mst_edges;

    // Map to store minimum edge weight to connect a vertex to the MST
    std::vector<long long> min_edge_weight(num_terminals, std::numeric_limits<long long>::max());
    // Vector to store the parent of each vertex in the MST
    std::vector<int> parent(num_terminals, -1);
    // Boolean array to track vertices included in MST
    std::vector<bool> in_mst(num_terminals, false);

    // Start with the first terminal (index 0)
    min_edge_weight[0] = 0;

    // The MST will have num_terminals vertices
    for (int count = 0; count < num_terminals; ++count) {
        int u_idx = -1;
        long long min_val = std::numeric_limits<long long>::max();

        // Find the vertex with the minimum edge weight among those not yet included in MST
        for (int v_idx = 0; v_idx < num_terminals; ++v_idx) {
            if (!in_mst[v_idx] && min_edge_weight[v_idx] < min_val) {
                min_val = min_edge_weight[v_idx];
                u_idx = v_idx;
            }
        }

        // If no vertex can be added, break (e.g., disconnected graph, though metric closure should be connected)
        if (u_idx == -1) break;

        in_mst[u_idx] = true;

        // Add the edge to the result if it's not the first vertex
        if (parent[u_idx] != -1) {
            result_mst_edges.push_back({terminals_map_idx_to_id[parent[u_idx]], terminals_map_idx_to_id[u_idx], (int)min_val});
        }

        // Update edge weights of adjacent vertices
        int u_terminal_id = terminals_map_idx_to_id[u_idx];
        if (metric_closure_adj.count(u_terminal_id)) {
            for (const auto& edge_pair : metric_closure_adj.at(u_terminal_id)) {
                int v_terminal_id = edge_pair.first;
                int weight = edge_pair.second;

                // Find the index of v_terminal_id in terminals_map_idx_to_id
                int v_idx = -1;
                for(int i = 0; i < terminals_map_idx_to_id.size(); ++i) {
                    if (terminals_map_idx_to_id[i] == v_terminal_id) {
                        v_idx = i;
                        break;
                    }
                }

                if (v_idx != -1 && !in_mst[v_idx] && weight < min_edge_weight[v_idx]) {
                    parent[v_idx] = u_idx;
                    min_edge_weight[v_idx] = weight;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return result_mst_edges;
}


int main() {
    // Fast I/O
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int num_vertices, num_edges, num_terminals;
    std::vector<std::vector<Edge>> adj;
    std::vector<int> terminals;

    parse_input(num_vertices, num_edges, num_terminals, adj, terminals);

    if (num_terminals <= 1) {
        // If 0 or 1 terminal, the Steiner tree is trivial
        if (num_terminals == 1) {
            std::cout << "0 0\n"; // Weight 0, 0 edges
        } else {
            std::cout << "0 0\n";
        }
        return 0;
    }

    // Store shortest path distances and predecessors from each terminal
    std::vector<std::vector<long long>> terminal_dists(num_terminals, std::vector<long long>(num_vertices));
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> terminal_preds(num_terminals, std::vector<int>(num_vertices));
    
    // Map to create a compact index for terminals in Prim's
    // Key: terminal ID, Value: index (0 to num_terminals-1)
    std::map<int, int> terminal_id_to_idx;
    // Vector to map index back to terminal ID
    std::vector<int> terminals_map_idx_to_id(num_terminals);

    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        terminal_id_to_idx[terminals[i]] = i;
        terminals_map_idx_to_id[i] = terminals[i];
        auto result = dijkstra(terminals[i], num_vertices, adj);
        terminal_dists[i] = result.first;
        terminal_preds[i] = result.second;
    }

    // Build the metric closure graph
    // Adjacency list for the metric closure graph: map terminal ID to list of {neighbor_terminal_ID, weight}
    std::map<int, std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>> metric_closure_adj;
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        for (int j = i + 1; j < num_terminals; ++j) {
            int u_term = terminals[i];
            int v_term = terminals[j];
            long long dist = terminal_dists[i][v_term]; // Distance from terminal i to terminal j

            if (dist != std::numeric_limits<long long>::max()) {
                metric_closure_adj[u_term].push_back({v_term, (int)dist});
                metric_closure_adj[v_term].push_back({u_term, (int)dist});
            }
        }
    }

    // Compute MST on the metric closure graph using Prim's algorithm
    std::vector<MSTEdge> metric_mst = prim_mst(num_terminals, metric_closure_adj, terminals_map_idx_to_id);

    // Collect all unique edges from the paths corresponding to the MST edges
    std::set<std::tuple<int, int, int>> steiner_tree_edges; // {u, v, weight}, sorted u, v
    
    for (const auto& mst_edge : metric_mst) {
        int u_term = mst_edge.u;
        int v_term = mst_edge.v;
        
        // Find which terminal index corresponds to u_term and v_term
        int u_term_idx = terminal_id_to_idx[u_term];
        // int v_term_idx = terminal_id_to_idx[v_term]; // Not directly used in reconstruct_path call

        // Reconstruct path using the correct predecessor array (based on which terminal was source)
        // The distance from u_term to v_term is given by terminal_dists[u_term_idx][v_term].
        // The predecessors for this path are stored in terminal_preds[u_term_idx].
        reconstruct_path(u_term, v_term, terminal_preds[u_term_idx], adj, steiner_tree_edges, terminal_dists[u_term_idx]);
    }

    // Union of edges might contain cycles. Compute a final MST on these collected edges.
    // First, collect all unique vertices involved in the steiner_tree_edges
    std::set<int> steiner_vertices_set;
    for (const auto& edge_tuple : steiner_tree_edges) {
        steiner_vertices_set.insert(std::get<0>(edge_tuple));
        steiner_vertices_set.insert(std::get<1>(edge_tuple));
    }
    
    // Convert set of vertices to a vector and create a map for new indices for MST
    std::vector<int> steiner_vertices(steiner_vertices_set.begin(), steiner_vertices_set.end());
    std::map<int, int> steiner_vertex_id_to_idx;
    for(int i = 0; i < steiner_vertices.size(); ++i) {
        steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[steiner_vertices[i]] = i;
    }

    // Build adjacency list for the subgraph formed by steiner_tree_edges
    // This is not directly used in Kruskal's but can be useful for debugging or other MST algos.
    // std::vector<std::vector<Edge>> steiner_subgraph_adj(steiner_vertices.size()); // Not needed for Kruskal's implementation below
    std::vector<MSTEdge> final_mst_candidate_edges;
    for (const auto& edge_tuple : steiner_tree_edges) {
        int u = std::get<0>(edge_tuple);
        int v = std::get<1>(edge_tuple);
        int weight = std::get<2>(edge_tuple);
        
        // int u_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[u]; // Not needed for Kruskal's candidate edges
        // int v_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[v]; // Not needed for Kruskal's candidate edges
        
        // steiner_subgraph_adj[u_idx].push_back({v_idx, weight}); // Not needed
        // steiner_subgraph_adj[v_idx].push_back({u_idx, weight}); // Not needed
        final_mst_candidate_edges.push_back({u, v, weight});
    }

    // Sort edges for Kruskal's algorithm
    std::sort(final_mst_candidate_edges.begin(), final_mst_candidate_edges.end(), compareMSTEdges);

    // Kruskal's algorithm for the final MST
    // Disjoint Set Union (DSU) structure
    std::vector<int> dsu_parent(steiner_vertices.size());
    std::vector<int> dsu_rank(steiner_vertices.size(), 0);
    for(int i = 0; i < steiner_vertices.size(); ++i) {
        dsu_parent[i] = i;
    }

    auto find_set = [&](auto self, int v_idx) -> int {
        if (v_idx == dsu_parent[v_idx])
            return v_idx;
        return dsu_parent[v_idx] = self(self, dsu_parent[v_idx]);
    };

    auto union_sets = [&](int a_idx, int b_idx) -> bool {
        a_idx = find_set(find_set, a_idx);
        b_idx = find_set(find_set, b_idx);
        if (a_idx != b_idx) {
            if (dsu_rank[a_idx] < dsu_rank[b_idx]) std::swap(a_idx, b_idx);
            dsu_parent[b_idx] = a_idx;
            if (dsu_rank[a_idx] == dsu_rank[b_idx]) dsu_rank[a_idx]++;
            return true;
        }
        return false;
    };

    std::vector<MSTEdge> final_steiner_tree;
    long long total_weight = 0;
    int edges_count = 0;

    for (const auto& edge : final_mst_candidate_edges) {
        int u_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[edge.u];
        int v_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[edge.v];

        if (union_sets(u_idx, v_idx)) {
            final_steiner_tree.push_back(edge);
            total_weight += edge.weight;
            edges_count++;
        }
    }

    // The problem statement guarantees a connected graph for terminals.
    // The resulting graph should connect all terminals.

    // Output
    std::cout << total_weight << " " << edges_count << "\n";
    for (const auto& edge : final_steiner_tree) {
        std::cout << edge.u << " " << edge.v << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

Approach: Here is the complete C++ code for solving the Steiner Tree problem using the MST heuristic:

View C++ Code (13253 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <limits>
#include <algorithm>
#include <set>
#include <map> // For metric closure graph

// Structure to represent an edge in the original graph
struct Edge {
    int to;
    int weight;
};

// Structure to represent a node in the priority queue for Dijkstra's
struct State {
    int vertex;
    int cost;

    bool operator>(const State& other) const {
        return cost > other.cost;
    }
};

// Function to parse input and build the graph
void parse_input(int& num_vertices, int& num_edges, int& num_terminals,
                 std::vector<std::vector<Edge>>& adj, std::vector<int>& terminals) {
    std::cin >> num_vertices >> num_edges >> num_terminals;

    adj.resize(num_vertices);
    for (int i = 0; i < num_edges; ++i) {
        int u, v, weight;
        std::cin >> u >> v >> weight;
        adj[u].push_back({v, weight});
        adj[v].push_back({u, weight}); // Undirected graph
    }

    terminals.resize(num_terminals);
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        std::cin >> terminals[i];
    }
}

// Dijkstra's algorithm to find shortest paths from a source vertex
// Returns distances and predecessors
std::pair<std::vector<long long>, std::vector<int>> dijkstra(int start_node, int num_vertices, const std::vector<std::vector<Edge>>& adj) {
    std::vector<long long> dist(num_vertices, std::numeric_limits<long long>::max());
    std::vector<int> pred(num_vertices, -1);
    std::priority_queue<State, std::vector<State>, std::greater<State>> pq;

    dist[start_node] = 0;
    pq.push({start_node, 0});

    while (!pq.empty()) {
        State current = pq.top();
        pq.pop();

        int u = current.vertex;
        int d = current.cost;

        if (d > dist[u]) {
            continue;
        }

        for (const auto& edge : adj[u]) {
            int v = edge.to;
            int weight = edge.weight;
            if (dist[u] + weight < dist[v]) {
                dist[v] = dist[u] + weight;
                pred[v] = u;
                pq.push({v, (int)dist[v]});
            }
        }
    }
    return {dist, pred};
}

// Function to reconstruct the path from start to end using predecessors
// Stores edges in the `path_edges` set. Edges are stored as pairs of {min_vertex, max_vertex} to handle undirected nature.
void reconstruct_path(int start, int end, const std::vector<int>& pred,
                      const std::vector<std::vector<Edge>>& adj,
                      std::set<std::tuple<int, int, int>>& path_edges,
                      const std::vector<long long>& dist) {
    if (dist[end] == std::numeric_limits<long long>::max()) {
        // No path exists
        return;
    }

    int current = end;
    while (current != start && current != -1) {
        int p = pred[current];
        if (p == -1) break; // Should not happen if dist[end] is not max

        // Find the weight of the edge (p, current)
        int weight = -1;
        for (const auto& edge : adj[p]) {
            if (edge.to == current) {
                weight = edge.weight;
                break;
            }
        }
        // Ensure edge is added in a canonical form (smaller vertex first)
        path_edges.insert({std::min(p, current), std::max(p, current), weight});
        current = p;
    }
}

// Structure for MST edges
struct MSTEdge {
    int u, v, weight;
};

// Comparator for MST edges for sorting (used in Kruskal's or Prim's with vector)
bool compareMSTEdges(const MSTEdge& a, const MSTEdge& b) {
    return a.weight < b.weight;
}

// Prim's algorithm for Minimum Spanning Tree
// Takes an adjacency list representation of the graph and returns a vector of MST edges.
// `metric_closure_adj` maps terminal ID to its neighbors and weights.
std::vector<MSTEdge> prim_mst(int num_terminals, const std::map<int, std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>>& metric_closure_adj, const std::vector<int>& terminals_map_idx_to_id) {
    std::vector<MSTEdge> result_mst_edges;
    if (num_terminals == 0) return result_mst_edges;

    // Map to store minimum edge weight to connect a vertex to the MST
    std::vector<long long> min_edge_weight(num_terminals, std::numeric_limits<long long>::max());
    // Vector to store the parent of each vertex in the MST
    std::vector<int> parent(num_terminals, -1);
    // Boolean array to track vertices included in MST
    std::vector<bool> in_mst(num_terminals, false);

    // Start with the first terminal (index 0)
    min_edge_weight[0] = 0;

    // The MST will have num_terminals vertices
    for (int count = 0; count < num_terminals; ++count) {
        int u_idx = -1;
        long long min_val = std::numeric_limits<long long>::max();

        // Find the vertex with the minimum edge weight among those not yet included in MST
        for (int v_idx = 0; v_idx < num_terminals; ++v_idx) {
            if (!in_mst[v_idx] && min_edge_weight[v_idx] < min_val) {
                min_val = min_edge_weight[v_idx];
                u_idx = v_idx;
            }
        }

        // If no vertex can be added, break (e.g., disconnected graph, though metric closure should be connected)
        if (u_idx == -1) break;

        in_mst[u_idx] = true;

        // Add the edge to the result if it's not the first vertex
        if (parent[u_idx] != -1) {
            result_mst_edges.push_back({terminals_map_idx_to_id[parent[u_idx]], terminals_map_idx_to_id[u_idx], (int)min_val});
        }

        // Update edge weights of adjacent vertices
        int u_terminal_id = terminals_map_idx_to_id[u_idx];
        if (metric_closure_adj.count(u_terminal_id)) {
            for (const auto& edge_pair : metric_closure_adj.at(u_terminal_id)) {
                int v_terminal_id = edge_pair.first;
                int weight = edge_pair.second;

                // Find the index of v_terminal_id in terminals_map_idx_to_id
                int v_idx = -1;
                for(int i = 0; i < terminals_map_idx_to_id.size(); ++i) {
                    if (terminals_map_idx_to_id[i] == v_terminal_id) {
                        v_idx = i;
                        break;
                    }
                }

                if (v_idx != -1 && !in_mst[v_idx] && weight < min_edge_weight[v_idx]) {
                    parent[v_idx] = u_idx;
                    min_edge_weight[v_idx] = weight;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return result_mst_edges;
}


int main() {
    // Fast I/O
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    int num_vertices, num_edges, num_terminals;
    std::vector<std::vector<Edge>> adj;
    std::vector<int> terminals;

    parse_input(num_vertices, num_edges, num_terminals, adj, terminals);

    if (num_terminals <= 1) {
        // If 0 or 1 terminal, the Steiner tree is trivial
        if (num_terminals == 1) {
            std::cout << "0 0\n"; // Weight 0, 0 edges
        } else {
            std::cout << "0 0\n";
        }
        return 0;
    }

    // Store shortest path distances and predecessors from each terminal
    std::vector<std::vector<long long>> terminal_dists(num_terminals, std::vector<long long>(num_vertices));
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> terminal_preds(num_terminals, std::vector<int>(num_vertices));
    
    // Map to create a compact index for terminals in Prim's
    // Key: terminal ID, Value: index (0 to num_terminals-1)
    std::map<int, int> terminal_id_to_idx;
    // Vector to map index back to terminal ID
    std::vector<int> terminals_map_idx_to_id(num_terminals);

    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        terminal_id_to_idx[terminals[i]] = i;
        terminals_map_idx_to_id[i] = terminals[i];
        auto result = dijkstra(terminals[i], num_vertices, adj);
        terminal_dists[i] = result.first;
        terminal_preds[i] = result.second;
    }

    // Build the metric closure graph
    // Adjacency list for the metric closure graph: map terminal ID to list of {neighbor_terminal_ID, weight}
    std::map<int, std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>> metric_closure_adj;
    for (int i = 0; i < num_terminals; ++i) {
        for (int j = i + 1; j < num_terminals; ++j) {
            int u_term = terminals[i];
            int v_term = terminals[j];
            long long dist = terminal_dists[i][v_term]; // Distance from terminal i to terminal j

            if (dist != std::numeric_limits<long long>::max()) {
                metric_closure_adj[u_term].push_back({v_term, (int)dist});
                metric_closure_adj[v_term].push_back({u_term, (int)dist});
            }
        }
    }

    // Compute MST on the metric closure graph using Prim's algorithm
    std::vector<MSTEdge> metric_mst = prim_mst(num_terminals, metric_closure_adj, terminals_map_idx_to_id);

    // Collect all unique edges from the paths corresponding to the MST edges
    std::set<std::tuple<int, int, int>> steiner_tree_edges; // {u, v, weight}, sorted u, v
    
    for (const auto& mst_edge : metric_mst) {
        int u_term = mst_edge.u;
        int v_term = mst_edge.v;
        
        // Find which terminal index corresponds to u_term and v_term
        int u_term_idx = terminal_id_to_idx[u_term];
        // int v_term_idx = terminal_id_to_idx[v_term]; // Not directly used in reconstruct_path call

        // Reconstruct path using the correct predecessor array (based on which terminal was source)
        // The distance from u_term to v_term is given by terminal_dists[u_term_idx][v_term].
        // The predecessors for this path are stored in terminal_preds[u_term_idx].
        reconstruct_path(u_term, v_term, terminal_preds[u_term_idx], adj, steiner_tree_edges, terminal_dists[u_term_idx]);
    }

    // Union of edges might contain cycles. Compute a final MST on these collected edges.
    // First, collect all unique vertices involved in the steiner_tree_edges
    std::set<int> steiner_vertices_set;
    for (const auto& edge_tuple : steiner_tree_edges) {
        steiner_vertices_set.insert(std::get<0>(edge_tuple));
        steiner_vertices_set.insert(std::get<1>(edge_tuple));
    }
    
    // Convert set of vertices to a vector and create a map for new indices for MST
    std::vector<int> steiner_vertices(steiner_vertices_set.begin(), steiner_vertices_set.end());
    std::map<int, int> steiner_vertex_id_to_idx;
    for(int i = 0; i < steiner_vertices.size(); ++i) {
        steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[steiner_vertices[i]] = i;
    }

    // Build adjacency list for the subgraph formed by steiner_tree_edges
    // This is not directly used in Kruskal's but can be useful for debugging or other MST algos.
    // std::vector<std::vector<Edge>> steiner_subgraph_adj(steiner_vertices.size()); // Not needed for Kruskal's implementation below
    std::vector<MSTEdge> final_mst_candidate_edges;
    for (const auto& edge_tuple : steiner_tree_edges) {
        int u = std::get<0>(edge_tuple);
        int v = std::get<1>(edge_tuple);
        int weight = std::get<2>(edge_tuple);
        
        // int u_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[u]; // Not needed for Kruskal's candidate edges
        // int v_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[v]; // Not needed for Kruskal's candidate edges
        
        // steiner_subgraph_adj[u_idx].push_back({v_idx, weight}); // Not needed
        // steiner_subgraph_adj[v_idx].push_back({u_idx, weight}); // Not needed
        final_mst_candidate_edges.push_back({u, v, weight});
    }

    // Sort edges for Kruskal's algorithm
    std::sort(final_mst_candidate_edges.begin(), final_mst_candidate_edges.end(), compareMSTEdges);

    // Kruskal's algorithm for the final MST
    // Disjoint Set Union (DSU) structure
    std::vector<int> dsu_parent(steiner_vertices.size());
    std::vector<int> dsu_rank(steiner_vertices.size(), 0);
    for(int i = 0; i < steiner_vertices.size(); ++i) {
        dsu_parent[i] = i;
    }

    auto find_set = [&](auto self, int v_idx) -> int {
        if (v_idx == dsu_parent[v_idx])
            return v_idx;
        return dsu_parent[v_idx] = self(self, dsu_parent[v_idx]);
    };

    auto union_sets = [&](int a_idx, int b_idx) -> bool {
        a_idx = find_set(find_set, a_idx);
        b_idx = find_set(find_set, b_idx);
        if (a_idx != b_idx) {
            if (dsu_rank[a_idx] < dsu_rank[b_idx]) std::swap(a_idx, b_idx);
            dsu_parent[b_idx] = a_idx;
            if (dsu_rank[a_idx] == dsu_rank[b_idx]) dsu_rank[a_idx]++;
            return true;
        }
        return false;
    };

    std::vector<MSTEdge> final_steiner_tree;
    long long total_weight = 0;
    int edges_count = 0;

    for (const auto& edge : final_mst_candidate_edges) {
        int u_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[edge.u];
        int v_idx = steiner_vertex_id_to_idx[edge.v];

        if (union_sets(u_idx, v_idx)) {
            final_steiner_tree.push_back(edge);
            total_weight += edge.weight;
            edges_count++;
        }
    }

    // The problem statement guarantees a connected graph for terminals.
    // The resulting graph should connect all terminals.

    // Output
    std::cout << total_weight << " " << edges_count << "\n";
    for (const auto& edge : final_steiner_tree) {
        std::cout << edge.u << " " << edge.v << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 33: Quadratic Assignment Problem (C#) Score: 0.00 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to solve the Quadratic Assignment Problem (QAP), which is NP-hard.

**Problem:**
You have N facilities and N locations. You must assign each facility to exactly one
location (a permutation). The cost of assigning facility i to location p[i] is:

sum over all pairs (i, j): flow[i][j] * distance[p[i]][p[j]]

Your goal is to minimize this total cost.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
N
flow[0][0] flow[0][1] ... flow[0][N-1]
...
flow[N-1][0] ... flow[N-1][N-1]
dist[0][0] dist[0][1] ... dist[0][N-1]
...
dist[N-1][0] ... dist[N-1][N-1]
```
All values are integers. Matrices are N×N. Indices are 0-based in the output.

**Output format (stdout):**
```
total_cost
p0 p1 p2 ... p(N-1)
```
Where p is a permutation of 0..N-1 and p[i] is the location assigned to facility i.

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Output a valid permutation.
3. Handle N from very small up to ~2000 within 5 minutes.
4. Heuristics are acceptable for large N; lower cost scores higher.

Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method. Assume only the standard library.

Assign N facilities to N locations so that the total cost is minimised, where cost depends on how much material flows between each pair of facilities multiplied by the distance between their assigned locations. Think of arranging departments in an office to minimise how far people walk between them.

This is the Quadratic Assignment Problem, one of the hardest combinatorial optimisation problems. The test uses planted instances with a known optimum for strict grading. Subpasses increase the problem size.

Test 34: Vehicle Routing Problem (Python) Score: 0.00 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Python code to solve the Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem (CVRP).

**Problem:**
You have a depot and N customers with coordinates and demands. You must create up to K vehicle
routes starting and ending at the depot. Each customer must be visited exactly once and the sum
of demands on any route must not exceed the vehicle capacity. Minimize total travel distance.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
N K CAPACITY
depot_x depot_y
x1 y1 demand1
x2 y2 demand2
...
xN yN demandN
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
total_distance
num_routes
route_1
route_2
...
```
Each route line should list customer indices (0-based) in visit order.

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Output valid routes that cover all customers exactly once.
3. Respect vehicle capacity on every route.
4. Heuristics are acceptable for large instances; shorter distance scores higher.

Write complete, runnable Python code.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Plan delivery routes for a fleet of vehicles so that every customer is visited and the total distance travelled is minimised. Each vehicle has a capacity limit, so the AI must decide which customers go on which truck and in what order.

This is the Vehicle Routing Problem, a generalisation of the Travelling Salesman Problem. Subpasses increase the number of customers and vehicles. The baseline uses a simple nearest-neighbour heuristic.

Test 35: Minimum Dominating Set (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 21
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the Minimum Dominating Set problem (NP-hard).

**Problem:**
Given an undirected graph, find a small set of vertices S such that every vertex is
either in S or adjacent to at least one vertex in S. Smaller sets score better.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u1 v1
u2 v2
...
```
Vertices are 0-indexed.

**Output format (stdout):**
```
set_size
v1 v2 v3 ...
```
Where the second line lists the vertices in the dominating set (0-indexed).

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Output a valid dominating set.
3. Heuristics are acceptable for large graphs; smaller sets score higher.
4. Must run within 5 minutes.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler information**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Test 36: Maximum Independent Set (C++) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve the **Maximum Independent Set** problem.

Given an undirected graph, find the largest set of vertices such that no two chosen vertices share an edge.

The evaluator is strict: only an exact maximum independent set is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
u v
u v
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` = number of vertices (0-indexed: 0..n-1)
- `m` = number of undirected edges
- each edge appears as two integers `u v`

**Output format (stdout)**
```
k
v1 v2 v3 ... vk
```
- `k` = size of your independent set
- second line lists exactly `k` distinct vertex indices (space-separated)
- vertices may be printed in any order

**What will be checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Every printed vertex is in range.
3. No edge exists between any two printed vertices.
4. `k` is the true maximum for the input graph.

**Requirements**
1. Use standard C++ (C++17-compatible).
2. Read from stdin and write to stdout.
3. Handle small and very large graphs.
4. Finish within the test timeout.

**Environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

This is NP-hard. The test uses planted instances with a known optimal answer for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the graph size.

Test 37: Feedback Vertex Set (C#) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to solve the **Directed Feedback Vertex Set** problem.

Given a directed graph, find the smallest set of vertices to remove so the remaining graph has **no directed cycles** (i.e., it becomes a DAG).

The evaluator is strict: only a minimum-size valid feedback vertex set is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
u v
u v
... (m lines total)
```
- `n`: number of vertices (0-indexed: `0..n-1`)
- `m`: number of directed edges
- each line `u v` means an edge `u -> v`

**Output format (stdout)**
```
k
v1 v2 v3 ... vk
```
- `k`: number of removed vertices
- second line: exactly `k` distinct vertex indices (any order)

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Vertex indices are valid and distinct.
3. Removing those vertices makes the graph acyclic.
4. The set size is minimum.

**Requirements**
1. Provide complete C# code with `Main`.
2. Read from stdin and write to stdout.
3. Handle small and very large graphs.
4. Finish within the test timeout.

**Environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB

SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

Test 38: Exact Cover (Python) Score: 0.00 / 16
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Python code to solve the **Exact Cover** problem.

Given a universe of elements and a list of sets, choose set indices such that:
1) every element is covered, and
2) no element is covered more than once.

The evaluator is strict: only the exact expected solution is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
U S
e1 e2 e3 ...
e1 e2 ...
... (S lines total)
```
- `U` = number of elements in the universe (elements are `0..U-1`)
- `S` = number of available sets
- each following line is one set, listed as space-separated element IDs

**Output format (stdout)**
```
SOLUTION
i1 i2 i3 ... ik
```
or
```
NO SOLUTION
```

- `i1..ik` are 0-based set indices into the input list.

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Indices are valid and distinct.
3. Chosen indices are exactly the planted exact-cover solution.

Write complete Python code that reads stdin and writes stdout.

Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt

View chain of thought

Given a collection of sets, choose some of them so that every element in the universe appears in exactly one chosen set — no gaps and no overlaps. Think of tiling a floor with pre-cut pieces where every tile must be used exactly once and every square must be covered.

This is a classic NP-complete problem. Each test instance has exactly one valid solution, so grading is strict pass/fail. Subpasses increase the universe and set sizes.

Test 39: Graph Bisection (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the **Graph Bisection** problem.

Given an undirected graph with an even number of vertices, split the vertices into two equal-size parts to minimize cut edges (edges with endpoints in different parts).

The evaluator is strict: only a minimum-cut valid bisection is accepted.

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
u v
u v
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` vertices labeled `0..n-1` (n is even)
- `m` undirected edges

**Output format (stdout)**
```
cut_size
v1 v2 v3 ... v(n/2)
```
- first line: reported cut size
- second line: vertices in partition A (exactly n/2 distinct indices)
- partition B is all remaining vertices

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. Partition size is exactly `n/2` and vertices are valid/distinct.
3. Reported cut matches the actual cut.
4. The cut is minimum for the instance.

Write complete Rust code with `main` that reads stdin and writes stdout.

Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Split a graph’s nodes into two equally-sized groups so that as few edges as possible cross between the groups. Think of dividing a school into two teams where friends want to stay together — minimise the number of friendships that get split.

The test uses planted graphs that can be perfectly bisected with zero crossing edges, so grading is strict pass/fail. Subpasses increase the graph size.

Test 40: Integer Linear Programming (C++) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve a **0/1 Integer Linear Program**.

Maximize:
sum(c_i * x_i)

Subject to:
x_u + x_v <= 1 (for each constraint pair u, v)
x_i in {0, 1}

**Input format (stdin)**
```
n m
c_0 c_1 ... c_(n-1)
u_0 v_0
u_1 v_1
... (m lines total)
```
- `n` = number of binary variables
- `m` = number of pair constraints

**Output format (stdout)**
```
objective_value
x_0 x_1 ... x_(n-1)
```

**What is checked**
1. Output format is valid.
2. `x_i` are binary and satisfy all constraints.
3. Reported objective matches the assignment.
4. Assignment achieves the true optimum for the instance.

This is 0/1 Integer Linear Programming, a fundamental NP-hard optimisation problem. The test uses planted instances with a known unique optimum for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the number of variables and constraints.

Test 41: Fragment Shaders (SPIR-V Assembly) Score: 0.00 / 20
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a SPIR-V assembly text fragment shader. The shader will be assembled
with spirv-as (Vulkan 1.0 target) and rendered on a unit sphere.

**Available inputs (from vertex shader):**
```
layout(location = 0) in vec3 worldPos; ; world-space position
layout(location = 1) in vec3 normal; ; world-space normal (interpolated, may need normalize)
layout(location = 2) in vec2 uv; ; texture coordinates in [0,1]
layout(location = 3) in vec3 tangent; ; tangent vector
layout(location = 4) in vec3 color; ; per-vertex color (smoothly varies: typical value: rgb = 0.5+0.5*xyz)
```

**Available uniform buffer (DescriptorSet 0, Binding 0):**
```
struct UBO { // std140 layout
mat4 model; // offset 0 (column-major)
mat4 view; // offset 64
mat4 projection; // offset 128
vec4 lightPos; // offset 192 -- typical value: (5.0, 5.0, 5.0, 1.0)
vec4 cameraPos; // offset 208 -- typical value: (0.0, 0.0, 3.0, 1.0)
vec4 params; // offset 224 -- typical value: (0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 0.0)
};
```

**Required output:**
```
layout(location = 0) out vec4 outColor; RGBA float, will be stored as rgba8unorm
```

**SPIR-V requirements:**
- Start with `; SPIR-V` header comment and version
- Use `OpCapability Shader` and `OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450`
- Use `OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main"` listing ALL input/output variables used
- Use `OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft`
- Declare all necessary decorations (Location, DescriptorSet, Binding, Block, Offsets)
- All input variables are `Input` storage class, output is `Output` storage class
- The uniform buffer variable is `Uniform` storage class
- Entry point function name must be "main"

**Write ONLY the complete SPIR-V assembly text.** Do not include any other text or explanation
outside the SPIR-V text.


**Task:** Output a solid red color (vec4(1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0)).
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Write GPU fragment shaders in raw SPIR-V assembly — the low-level instruction set that graphics cards actually execute. Each subpass asks for a different visual effect (lighting, patterns, fractals) rendered onto a 3D sphere, and the output image is compared pixel-by-pixel against a reference.

This is the hardest shader-language test because SPIR-V assembly is essentially GPU machine code: every operation, type declaration, and memory access must be spelled out by hand.

SPIR-V Shader - Normal to RGB

View SPIR-V (2258 chars)
; SPIR-V
SPIRV-Assembly-Version 1.0
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Types
%type.f32 = OpTypeFloat 32
%type.vec2.f32 = OpTypeVector %type.f32 2 ; For uv
%type.vec3.f32 = OpTypeVector %type.f32 3
%type.vec4.f32 = OpTypeVector %type.f32 4
%type.void = OpTypeVoid
%func_type_void = OpTypeFunction %type.void
%type.mat4.f32 = OpTypeMatrix %type.vec4.f32 4 ; GLSL mat4 is column-major, 4 columns of vec4
%type.ubo_ubo = OpTypeStruct %type.mat4.f32 %type.mat4.f32 %type.mat4.f32 %type.vec4.f32 %type.vec4.f32 %type.vec4.f32
%type.uniform_buffer.UBO = OpTypePointer Uniform %type.ubo_ubo

; Constants
%const_1_0 = OpConstant %type.f32 1.0
%const_0_5 = OpConstant %type.f32 0.5
%const_alpha_1_0 = OpConstant %type.f32 1.0

; Input Variables
%worldPos = OpVariable %type.vec3.f32 Input
    OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
%normal = OpVariable %type.vec3.f32 Input
    OpDecorate %normal Location 1
%uv = OpVariable %type.vec2.f32 Input
    OpDecorate %uv Location 2
%tangent = OpVariable %type.vec3.f32 Input
    OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
%color = OpVariable %type.vec3.f32 Input
    OpDecorate %color Location 4

; Output Variable
%outColor = OpVariable %type.vec4.f32 Output
    OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

; Uniform Buffer
%ubo = OpVariable %type.uniform_buffer.UBO Uniform
    OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
    OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0

; Entry Point
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos %normal %uv %tangent %color %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Function Definition
%main = OpFunction %type.void %func_type_void
%main_label = OpLabel

    ; Load input normal
    %normal_loaded = OpLoad %type.vec3.f32 %normal

    ; Normalize the normal (in case it's not unit length after interpolation)
    %normalized_normal = OpNormalize %type.vec3.f32 %normal_loaded

    ; Map normal from [-1, 1] to [0, 1] for RGB components
    %normal_plus_1 = OpFAdd %type.vec3.f32 %normalized_normal %const_1_0
    %color_rgb = OpFMul %type.vec3.f32 %normal_plus_1 %const_0_5

    ; Construct the final RGBA color, setting Alpha to 1.0
    %out_color_final = OpCompositeConstruct %type.vec4.f32 %color_rgb %const_alpha_1_0

    ; Store the output color
    OpStore %outColor %out_color_final

    OpReturn
    OpFunctionEnd

; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Unknown

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Input variables (from vertex shader)
OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %normal Location 1
OpDecorate %uv Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
OpDecorate %color Location 4

; Output variable
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

; Uniform buffer
OpDecorate %UBOBlock BufferBlock
OpDecorate %ub 0 Binding 0
OpDecorate %ub DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ub Offset 0
OpDecorate %ub ColMajor
OpDecorate %ub MatrixStride 16
OpDecorate %ub Stride 16

OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 0 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 0 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 1 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 1 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 2 ColMajor
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 2 MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBOBlock 5 Offset 224

%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4float = OpTypeMatrix %v4float 4

%UBO = OpTypeStruct %mat4float %mat4float %mat4float %v4float %v4float %v4float
%UBOBlock = OpTypePointer Uniform %UBO
%ub = OpConstantNull %UBOBlock

%void = OpTypeVoid
%func = OpTypeFunction %void

%worldPos = OpTypeVector %float 3
%worldPos = OpTypePointer Input %worldPos
%worldPos = OpVariable %worldPos

%normal = OpTypeVector %float 3
%normal = OpTypePointer Input %normal
%normal = OpVariable %normal

%uv = OpTypeVector %float 2
%uv = OpTypePointer Input %uv
%uv = OpVariable %uv

%tangent = OpTypeVector %float 3
%tangent = OpTypePointer Input %tangent
%tangent = OpVariable %tangent

%color = OpTypeVector %float 3
%color = OpTypePointer Input %color
%color = OpVariable %color

%outColor = OpTypeVector %float 4
%outColor = OpTypePointer Output %outColor
%outColor = OpVariable %outColor

%main = OpFunction %void %func
%label1 = OpLabel
%uniform_buffer = OpLoad %UBO %ub
%uv_var = OpLoad %uv %uv
%uv_x = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv_var 0
%uv_y = OpCompositeExtract %float %uv_var 1
%zero_float = OpConstant %float 0.0
%one_float = OpConstant %float 1.0
%color_vec = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %uv_x %uv_y %zero_float %one_float
OpStore %outColor %color_vec
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

View SPIR-V (2528 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Unknown
; Target: Vulkan 1.0

OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Entrypoint
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Input variables
OpDecorate %worldPos Input
OpDecorate %normal Input
OpDecorate %uv Input
OpDecorate %tangent Input
OpDecorate %color Input
OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %normal Location 1
OpDecorate %uv Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
OpDecorate %color Location 4

; Output variable
OpDecorate %outColor Output
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

; Uniform buffer
OpDecorate %_uniform_buffer Uniform
OpDecorate %_uniform_buffer Binding 0
OpDecorate %_uniform_buffer DescriptorSet 0
OpMemberDecorate %_uniform_buffer UniformBuffer
OpMemberDecorate %_uniform_buffer BufferStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %_uniform_buffer Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %_uniform_buffer MatrixStride 16
OpMemberDecorate %_uniform_ubo Ubo
OpMemberDecorate %_uniform_ubo Offset 0

%_runtime_array_ubo = OpTypeRuntimeArray %_uniform_ubo
%_uniform_buffer_type = OpTypeStruct %_runtime_array_ubo
%_uniform_buffer = OpVariable %_uniform_buffer_type Uniform

%_uniform_ubo = OpTypeStruct ; UBO struct
%_uniform_ubo_model = OpTypeMatrix %_f32 4 4
%_uniform_ubo_view = OpTypeMatrix %_f32 4 4
%_uniform_ubo_projection = OpTypeMatrix %_f32 4 4
%_uniform_ubo_lightPos = OpTypeVector %_f32 4
%_uniform_ubo_cameraPos = OpTypeVector %_f32 4
%_uniform_ubo_params = OpTypeVector %_f32 4

%_uniform_ubo = OpTypeStruct %_uniform_ubo_model %_uniform_ubo_view %_uniform_ubo_projection %_uniform_ubo_lightPos %_uniform_ubo_cameraPos %_uniform_ubo_params

; Basic types
%_void = OpTypeVoid
%_f32 = OpTypeFloat 32
%_vec3 = OpTypeVector %_f32 3
%_vec4 = OpTypeVector %_f32 4

; Input variable types
%_in_worldPos = OpTypeVector %_f32 3
%_in_normal = OpTypeVector %_f32 3
%_in_uv = OpTypeVector %_f32 2
%_in_tangent = OpTypeVector %_f32 3
%_in_color = OpTypeVector %_f32 4

; Output variable type
%_outColor = OpTypeVector %_f32 4

; Main function
%main = OpFunction %_void None %_
  %label = OpLabel
    ; Load inputs
    %worldPos = OpLoad %_vec3 %worldPos
    %normal = OpLoad %_vec3 %normal
    %uv = OpLoad %_vec2 %uv
    %tangent = OpLoad %_vec3 %tangent
    %color = OpLoad %_vec4 %color

    ; Construct the output color: per-vertex color with alpha 1.0
    %const_alpha = OpConstant %_f32 1.0
    %outColor = OpCompositeConstruct %_vec4 %color %const_alpha

    ; Store output
    OpStore %outColor %outColor

    OpReturn
  OpFunctionEnd

View SPIR-V (3460 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Khronos Glslang (Vulkan 1.0)
; Target: Vulkan 1.0
; SPIR-V requirements: OpCapability Shader, OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450, OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main", OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft
; Decorations: Location, DescriptorSet, Binding, Block, Offsets, Input, Output, Uniform

; Capabilities
OpCapability Shader

; Memory Model
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Entry Point
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos %normal %uv %tangent %color %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Input variables from vertex shader
OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
OpTypeVector %worldPosType 3 Float
OpVariable %worldPosType %worldPos_var %worldPos

OpDecorate %normal Location 1
OpTypeVector %normalType 3 Float
OpVariable %normalType %normal_var %normal

OpDecorate %uv Location 2
OpTypeVector %uvType 2 Float
OpVariable %uvType %uv_var %uv

OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
OpTypeVector %tangentType 3 Float
OpVariable %tangentType %tangent_var %tangent

OpDecorate %color Location 4
OpTypeVector %colorType 3 Float
OpVariable %colorType %color_var %color

; Output variable
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0
OpTypeVector %outColorType 4 Float
OpVariable %outColorType %outColor_var %outColor

; Uniform buffer
OpDecorate %ubo UboLayout
OpDecorate %ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo Binding 0
OpMemberDecorate %UboLayout 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UboLayout 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UboLayout 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UboLayout 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UboLayout 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UboLayout 5 Offset 224
OpTypeStruct %modelType %viewType %projectionType %lightPosType %cameraPosType %paramsType
OpTypeMatrix %model 4 Float
OpTypeMatrix %view 4 Float
OpTypeMatrix %projection 4 Float
OpTypeVector %lightPos 4 Float
OpTypeVector %cameraPos 4 Float
OpTypeVector %params 4 Float

OpVariable %UboLayout %ubo_var %ubo

; Main function
%main = OpFunction %void None %func_main
  %label_begin = OpLabel
    ; Load input variables
    %loaded_worldPos = OpLoad %worldPosType %worldPos_var
    %loaded_normal = OpLoad %normalType %normal_var
    %loaded_uv = OpLoad %uvType %uv_var
    %loaded_tangent = OpLoad %tangentType %tangent_var
    %loaded_color = OpLoad %colorType %color_var

    ; Load uniform buffer
    %loaded_ubo = OpLoad %UboLayout %ubo_var

    ; Extract light position from UBO
    %lightPos_full = OpAccessChain %lightPosType %loaded_ubo %uint(3) ; Index 3 for lightPos
    %lightPos_xyz = OpCompositeExtract %float3 %lightPos_full 0 1 2 ; Extract xyz

    ; Calculate L = normalize(lightPos.xyz - worldPos)
    %L_vec = OpVectorSubtract %float3 %lightPos_xyz %loaded_worldPos
    %L_norm_sq = OpDot %float %L_vec %L_vec ; Squared length for normalization
    %L_norm = OpSqrt %float %L_norm_sq
    %L = OpDiv %float3 %L_vec %L_norm ; Normalized L

    ; Calculate N = normalize(normal)
    %N_norm_sq = OpDot %float %loaded_normal %loaded_normal ; Squared length for normalization
    %N_norm = OpSqrt %float %N_norm_sq
    %N = OpDiv %float3 %loaded_normal %N_norm ; Normalized N

    ; Calculate d = max(dot(N,L),0)
    %dot_NL = OpDot %float %N %L
    %d = OpMax %float %dot_NL %float(0.0)

    ; Final color: d with alpha 1.0
    %outColor_final = OpCompositeConstruct %float4 %d %d %d %float(1.0) ; RGBA = (d, d, d, 1.0)

    ; Store the final color
    OpStore %outColor_var %outColor_final
  OpBranch %label_end

  %label_end = OpLabel
    OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

SPIR-V Shader - Blinn-Phong

View SPIR-V (3991 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Khronos Tools 1.3.220.0
; Bound: 39

OpCapability Shader
OpExtension "GL_EXT_shader_explicit_arithmetic_types"
OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; Types
%void = OpTypeVoid
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%vec2 = OpTypeVector %float 2
%vec3 = OpTypeVector %float 3
%vec4 = OpTypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %float 4

; UBO Type (std140 layout)
%UBO_Type = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %vec4 %vec4 %vec4
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_Type Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_Type Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_Type Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_Type Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_Type Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UBO_Type Offset 224

; Uniform buffer variable
%uniform_buffer = OpVariable Uniform %UBO_Type
OpDecorate %uniform_buffer DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %uniform_buffer Binding 0

; Input variables
%worldPos_var = OpVariable Input %vec3
OpDecorate %worldPos_var Location 0

%normal_var = OpVariable Input %vec3
OpDecorate %normal_var Location 1

%uv_var = OpVariable Input %vec2
OpDecorate %uv_var Location 2

%tangent_var = OpVariable Input %vec3
OpDecorate %tangent_var Location 3

%color_var = OpVariable Input %vec3
OpDecorate %color_var Location 4

; Output variable
%outColor_var = OpVariable Output %vec4
OpDecorate %outColor_var Location 0

; Constants
%float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.0
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.0
%float_64 = OpConstant %float 64.0
%float_0_1 = OpConstant %float 0.1
%float_0_6 = OpConstant %float 0.6
%float_0_8 = OpConstant %float 0.8
%glsl_std450 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"

; Entry point function
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos_var %normal_var %uv_var %tangent_var %color_var %outColor_var
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

%main = OpFunction %void %void_fn

%label_entry = OpLabel

; Load UBO members
%mat4_model = OpCompositeExtract %mat4 %uniform_buffer 0
%mat4_view = OpCompositeExtract %mat4 %uniform_buffer 1
%mat4_projection = OpCompositeExtract %mat4 %uniform_buffer 2
%vec4_lightPos = OpCompositeExtract %vec4 %uniform_buffer 3
%vec4_cameraPos = OpCompositeExtract %vec4 %uniform_buffer 4
; %vec4_params = OpCompositeExtract %vec4 %uniform_buffer 5 ; Not used

%lightPos_xyz = OpCompositeExtract %vec3 %vec4_lightPos 0
%cameraPos_xyz = OpCompositeExtract %vec3 %vec4_cameraPos 0

; Load input variables
%worldPos_val = OpLoad %vec3 %worldPos_var
%normal_val = OpLoad %vec3 %normal_var

; Calculate L (light direction)
; L = normalize(lightPos.xyz - worldPos)
%vec_light_dir = OpFSub %vec3 %lightPos_xyz %worldPos_val
%vec_L = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl_std450 Normalize %vec_light_dir

; Calculate V (view direction)
; V = normalize(cameraPos.xyz - worldPos)
%vec_view_dir = OpFSub %vec3 %cameraPos_xyz %worldPos_val
%vec_V = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl_std450 Normalize %vec_view_dir

; Calculate H (half vector)
; H = normalize(L + V)
%vec_L_plus_V = OpFAdd %vec3 %vec_L %vec_V
%vec_H = OpExtInst %vec3 %glsl_std450 Normalize %vec_L_plus_V

; Calculate diffuse component
; diffuse = max(dot(N, L), 0)
%dotNL = OpExtInst %float %glsl_std450 Dot %normal_val %vec_L
%diffuse = OpExtInst %float %glsl_std450 Max %dotNL %float_0

; Calculate specular component
; specular = pow(max(dot(N, H), 0), 64)
%dotNH = OpExtInst %float %glsl_std450 Dot %normal_val %vec_H
%specular_term = OpExtInst %float %glsl_std450 Max %dotNH %float_0
%specular = OpExtInst %float %glsl_std450 Pow %specular_term %float_64

; Combine components
; intensity = 0.1 + 0.6*diffuse + 0.8*specular
%term_diffuse = FMul %float %float_0_6 %diffuse
%term_specular = FMul %float %float_0_8 %specular
%intensity = FAdd %float %float_0_1 %term_diffuse
%intensity = FAdd %float %intensity %term_specular

; Construct output color
; outColor = vec4(intensity, intensity, intensity, 1.0)
%final_color = OpCompositeConstruct %vec4 %intensity %intensity %intensity %float_1

; Store output color
OpStore %outColor_var %final_color

; Return
OpBranch %label_end

%label_end = OpLabel
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

SPIR-V Shader - Rim Lighting

View SPIR-V (3877 chars)
; SPIR-V
OpCapability Shader
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %outColor %worldPos %normal %uv %tangent %color %Ubo
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

; Input variables
OpDecorate %worldPos Input
OpDecorate %worldPos Location 0
OpDecorate %normal Input
OpDecorate %normal Location 1
OpDecorate %uv Input
OpDecorate %uv Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent Input
OpDecorate %tangent Location 3
OpDecorate %color Input
OpDecorate %color Location 4

; Output variable
OpDecorate %outColor Output
OpDecorate %outColor Location 0

; Uniform buffer
OpDecorate %Ubo Binding 0
OpDecorate %Ubo DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %Ubo Block
OpMemberDecorate %type_Ubo 0 Offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %type_Ubo 1 Offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %type_Ubo 2 Offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %type_Ubo 3 Offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %type_Ubo 4 Offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %type_Ubo 5 Offset 224
OpDecorate %type_Ubo Block

%type_f32 = OpTypeFloat 32
%type_f32_vec3 = OpTypeVector %type_f32 3
%type_f32_vec4 = OpTypeVector %type_f32 4
%type_mat4 = OpTypeMatrix %type_f32 4

%type_Ubo = OpTypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %type_f32_vec4 %type_f32_vec4 %type_f32_vec4
%type_ptr_Ubo = OpTypePointer Uniform %type_Ubo
%Ubo = OpVariable %type_ptr_Ubo Uniform

; Function definitions
%main = OpFunction %type_void None %void
%label_begin = OpLabel
  %worldPos_load = OpLoad %type_f32_vec3 %worldPos
  %normal_load = OpLoad %type_f32_vec3 %normal
  %uv_load = OpLoad %type_f32_vec2 %uv
  %tangent_load = OpLoad %type_f32_vec3 %tangent
  %color_load = OpLoad %type_f32_vec3 %color

  %ubo_ptr_val = OpLoad %type_Ubo %Ubo
  %params = OpCompositeExtract %type_f32_vec4 %ubo_ptr_val 5

  %lightPos = OpCompositeExtract %type_f32_vec4 %ubo_ptr_val 3
  %cameraPos = OpCompositeExtract %type_f32_vec4 %ubo_ptr_val 4

  ; Base color (0.2, 0.3, 0.8)
  %baseColor = OpConstantComposite %type_f32_vec3 %type_f32 0.2 %type_f32 0.3 %type_f32 0.8
  
  ; Diffuse calculation (N dot L)
  %lightDir = OpLoad %type_f33_vec4 %lightPos 
  %lightDir_xyz = OpCompositeExtract %type_f32_vec3 %lightDir 0
  %normal_norm = OpCompositeNormalise %type_f32_vec3 %normal_load
  %lightDir_norm = OpCompositeNormalise %type_f32_vec3 %lightDir_xyz
  %diffuse = OpDot %type_f32 %normal_norm %lightDir_norm
  %diffuse_clamped = OpMax %type_f32 %diffuse %type_f32 0.0
  
  ; Modulate base color by (0.3 + 0.5*diffuse)
  %diffuse_factor = OpFAdd %type_f32 %type_f32 0.3 (%type_f32 0.5 * %diffuse_clamped)
  %diffuse_color = %baseColor * %diffuse_factor

  ; Rim lighting (Fresnel)
  ; N dot V: V is vector from fragment to camera. 
  ; Camera position is in UBO.worldPos.xyz
  %viewDir_raw = %cameraPos - %worldPos_load 
  %viewDir = OpCompositeNormalise %type_f32_vec3 %viewDir_raw
  %NdotV = OpDot %type_f32 %normal_norm %viewDir
  %rim_factor = OpPow %type_f32 (%type_f32 1.0 - OpMax %type_f32 %NdotV %type_f32 0.0) %type_f32 3.0
  
  ; Rim tint (1.0, 0.8, 0.5)
  %rimTint = OpConstantComposite %type_f32_vec3 %type_f32 1.0 %type_f32 0.8 %type_f32 0.5
  %rim_color = %rimTint * %rim_factor

  ; Final color: diffuse_color + rim_color
  %final_color_rgb = %diffuse_color + %rim_color
  %final_color_alpha = OpConstant %type_f32 1.0
  %outColor_val = OpCompositeComposite %type_f32_vec4 %final_color_rgb %final_color_alpha

  OpStore %outColor %outColor_val

  OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd

; Type definitions
%void = OpTypeVoid
%type_f32_vec2 = OpTypeVector %type_f32 2
%type_ptr_Output_vec4 = OpTypePointer Output %type_f32_vec4
%outColor = OpVariable %type_ptr_Output_vec4 Output
%type_ptr_Input_vec3 = OpTypePointer Input %type_f32_vec3
%worldPos = OpVariable %type_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%normal = OpVariable %type_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%type_ptr_Input_vec2 = OpTypePointer Input %type_f32_vec2
%uv = OpVariable %type_ptr_Input_vec2 Input
%tangent = OpVariable %type_ptr_Input_vec3 Input
%color = OpVariable %type_ptr_Input_vec3 Input

SPIR-V Shader - Toon Shading

View SPIR-V (3954 chars)
; SPIR-V
; Version: 1.0
; Generator: Gemini CLI;
; Target: Vulkan 1.0

OpCapability Shader
OpExtension "SPV_GOOGLE_hlsl_functionality1"
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450

; --- Constants ---
%float_0_0 = Constant %3 0.0
%float_0_2 = Constant %3 0.2
%float_0_25 = Constant %3 0.25
%float_0_4 = Constant %3 0.4
%float_0_5 = Constant %3 0.5
%float_0_7 = Constant %3 0.7
%float_0_75 = Constant %3 0.75
%float_1_0 = Constant %3 1.0

; --- Types ---
%void = TypeVoid
%bool = TypeBool
%float = TypeFloat 32
%int = TypeInt 32 0
%v2f = TypeVector %float 2
%v3f = TypeVector %float 3
%v4f = TypeVector %float 4
%mat4 = TypeMatrix %v4f 4

; UBO Struct Definition
%UboStruct = TypeStruct %mat4 %mat4 %mat4 %v4f %v4f %v4f
%ubo_ptr_type = TypePointer Uniform %UboStruct ; Pointer to UBO struct

; --- Variables ---
; Input Variables
%worldPos_in = Variable Input %v3f
%normal_in = Variable Input %v3f
%uv_in = Variable Input %v2f
%tangent_in = Variable Input %v3f
%color_in = Variable Input %v4f

; Output Variable
%outColor_out = Variable Output %v4f

; Uniform Buffer Variable
%ubo_buffer = Variable Uniform %UboStruct

; --- Entry Point Function ---
%main = Function %void None %{
  %label_entry = Label

  ; Load Input Variables
  %worldPos = Load %v3f %worldPos_in
  %normal = Load %v3f %normal_in
  %uv = Load %v2f %uv_in
  %tangent = Load %v3f %tangent_in
  %vertexColor = Load %v4f %color_in

  ; Load Uniform Buffer
  %ubo_access_chain = AccessChain %ubo_ptr_type %ubo_buffer
  %ubo = Load %UboStruct %ubo_access_chain
  %lightPos = CompositeExtract %v4f %ubo 3 ; offset 192

  ; --- Calculations ---
  ; Light Direction: L = normalize(worldPos - lightPos.xyz)
  %lightPos_xyz = CompositeExtract %v3f %lightPos 0
  %worldPos_minus_lightPos = Composite tristeza %v3f %worldPos %lightPos_xyz
  %lightDir = Normalize %v3f %worldPos_minus_lightPos

  ; Normalize Normal Vector
  %normal_norm = Normalize %v3f %normal

  ; Diffuse Term: d = max(dot(N, L), 0.0)
  %dot_nl = Dot %float %normal_norm %lightDir
  %diffuse = FMax %float %dot_nl %float_0_0

  ; Toon Shading Quantization
  ; Thresholds: 0.75, 0.5, 0.25
  ; Values: 1.0, 0.7, 0.4, 0.2

  ; Logic:
  ; if diffuse > 0.75: 1.0
  ; else if diffuse > 0.5: 0.7
  ; else if diffuse > 0.25: 0.4
  ; else: 0.2

  ; Use cascaded Select statements
  ; Start with the lowest threshold condition
  %cond_gt_025 = FOrdGreaterThan %bool %diffuse %float_0_25
  %banded_diffuse = Select %float %float_0_2 %float_0_4 %cond_gt_025 ; If diffuse > 0.25, use 0.4, else use 0.2

  %cond_gt_05 = FOrdGreaterThan %bool %diffuse %float_0_5
  %banded_diffuse = Select %float %banded_diffuse %float_0_7 %cond_gt_05 ; If diffuse > 0.5, use 0.7, else use current value (0.2 or 0.4)

  %cond_gt_075 = FOrdGreaterThan %bool %diffuse %float_0_75
  %banded_diffuse = Select %float %banded_diffuse %float_1_0 %cond_gt_075 ; If diffuse > 0.75, use 1.0, else use current value

  ; Multiply vertex color by diffuse band
  %final_color_rgb = FMul %v3f %banded_diffuse %vertexColor.xyz
  %final_color_rgba = CompositeConstruct %v4f %final_color_rgb %float_1_0 ; Output alpha is 1.0

  ; Store Output
  Store %v4f %final_color_rgba %outColor_out

  Return
%}

; --- Decorations ---
OpEntryPoint Fragment %main "main" %worldPos_in %normal_in %uv_in %tangent_in %color_in %outColor_out
OpExecutionMode %main OriginUpperLeft

OpDecorate %worldPos_in Location 0
OpDecorate %normal_in Location 1
OpDecorate %uv_in Location 2
OpDecorate %tangent_in Location 3
OpDecorate %color_in Location 4
OpDecorate %outColor_out Location 0

OpDecorate %UboStruct BufferBlock ; UBO struct is a buffer block
OpMemberDecorate %UboStruct %mat4 offset 0
OpMemberDecorate %UboStruct %mat4 offset 64
OpMemberDecorate %UboStruct %mat4 offset 128
OpMemberDecorate %UboStruct %v4f offset 192
OpMemberDecorate %UboStruct %v4f offset 208
OpMemberDecorate %UboStruct %v4f offset 224

OpDecorate %ubo_buffer DescriptorSet 0
OpDecorate %ubo_buffer Binding 0
; --- End of SPIR-V ---
